title: Allow to add image to manifest list without platform entry
body:
### Feature request description

For the new image volume feature added to Kubernetes 1.31 it would be great to be able to add images to a manifest without a platform. The primary use case for mounting OCI images is to access large data like AI models or immutable configuration sets. These are _passive data images_ which are only used read-only (no active components, so the OS architecture is irrelevant for those images).

As described in https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/issues/8781, mounting an OCI image created for a different architecture than the runtime's is an issue, although it shouldn't matter. 

The current solution is to _not use indexed manifests_ or to add a manifest with no platform entry. To help with the latter, it would be great if podman would support this.

### Suggest potential solution

A solution for the feature requested above would be to add an option `--no-platform` to `podman build`. When provided the build should happen on the actual platform (as if no `--platform` option is given), but the generated manifest entry should not contain the platform as all

I.e. 

instead of 

```
18:58 ➜ podman build --manifest docker.io/rhuss/caikit-flan-t5-small .
STEP 1/3: FROM busybox
WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64) does not match the expected platform (linux/arm64)
STEP 2/3: RUN mkdir /models && chmod 775 /models
--> Using cache b7a602724daf94766add862cfa0446a9d241fcca8a4a9df22d28d356018a1466
--> b7a602724daf
STEP 3/3: COPY flan-t5-small-caikit/ /models/
--> Using cache 1db290d23fed6195593abeb1a5bfb8a5d0013c5c2416031935babc29e0497675
--> 1db290d23fed
1db290d23fed6195593abeb1a5bfb8a5d0013c5c2416031935babc29e0497675

18:58 ➜ podman manifest inspect docker.io/rhuss/caikit-flan-t5-small
{
    "schemaVersion": 2,
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json",
    "manifests": [
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
            "size": 1031,
            "digest": "sha256:25fd85a60955641f02eaedd87b1b4090df549b2ecd436612b3173dfd224fb6e7",
            "platform": {
                "architecture": "amd64",
                "os": "linux"
            }
        }
    ]
}
```

one could also offer

```
18:58 ➜ podman build --no-platform --manifest docker.io/rhuss/caikit-flan-t5-small .
STEP 1/3: FROM busybox
WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64) does not match the expected platform (linux/arm64)
STEP 2/3: RUN mkdir /models && chmod 775 /models
--> Using cache b7a602724daf94766add862cfa0446a9d241fcca8a4a9df22d28d356018a1466
--> b7a602724daf
STEP 3/3: COPY flan-t5-small-caikit/ /models/
--> Using cache 1db290d23fed6195593abeb1a5bfb8a5d0013c5c2416031935babc29e0497675
--> 1db290d23fed
1db290d23fed6195593abeb1a5bfb8a5d0013c5c2416031935babc29e0497675

18:58 ➜ podman manifest inspect docker.io/rhuss/caikit-flan-t5-small
{
    "schemaVersion": 2,
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json",
    "manifests": [
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
            "size": 1031,
            "digest": "sha256:25fd85a60955641f02eaedd87b1b4090df549b2ecd436612b3173dfd224fb6e7"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Have you considered any alternatives?

No

### Additional context

In the KServe community, we plan to integrate image volumes to efficiently leverage the OCI image format for distributing huge AI models. See this issue for more context: https://github.com/kserve/kserve/issues/4083

We want to make it easy for data scientists to create such images and avoid pitfalls like described in https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/issues/8781 .

comments.body:
+1
and afaik some communities have settled for `unknown/unknown` for the os/architecture value tuple for these cases.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
